name: Podcast Clip
description: Long-form podcast / interview / panel / talk → 15-60s viral cut for TikTok / Reels / Shorts. Reference-required: a source long-form video URL or file MUST be supplied — there is no way to invent podcast content. Style discipline: title-banner with provocative hook quote at the top (12% of frame), karaoke captions sync'd to speech at the bottom (18%), dynamic 9:16 reframe (smart-crop on the active speaker), occasional B-roll cuts for variety, music typically OFF (diegetic VO carries) with optional low bed under the title-banner moment. One 60-90 minute podcast usually yields 2-4 short cuts that go viral. The tooling pipeline is end-to-end: yt-dlp pull → transcribe → Gemini-vision viral-moment picker → lossless extract → Remotion compose.

stackSummary.sourcePull: yt-dlp via `ralphy ref pull --url <yt|tiktok|ig|x|vimeo>` — downloads the long-form source into workspace/refs/<slug>/, with metadata + audio-extracted track for transcription.
stackSummary.transcription: ElevenLabs Scribe v1 via `ralphy ref transcribe <slug>` — word-level timestamps (ts_start, ts_end per token). This is what makes karaoke captions sync to the millisecond and what feeds the viral-moment picker context window.
stackSummary.viralMomentPicker: OpenRouter google/gemini-2.5-flash via `.agents/skills/ralph-researcher/scripts/find-viral-moments.ts` — ingests the transcript + sampled video frames, returns 4-8 candidate moments with (start, end, hook-quote, why-it-works) JSON. Cheap (≈$0.02-0.06 per 60-min source).
stackSummary.lossSelessCut: `ralphy video extract-segment <ref-slug> --start <ss> --end <ee> --pad-before 0.15 --pad-after 0.10 --out <project-id>/clips/cut-NN.mp4` — copy-codec ffmpeg cut, snapped to word boundaries from the transcript so no syllable is ever clipped.
stackSummary.reframe: src/lib/utils/smart-crop.ts — single-speaker face-tracker (mediapipe-style bbox per frame) drives a panning 9:16 viewport over the source 16:9. For 2-speaker debates, vertical split-screen via Remotion <Sequence>+<OffthreadVideo>.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs Scribe v1 word-level timestamps consumed by KaraokeCaptions (Inter Bold 50px, bottom 18%, word-by-word fill). HormoziCaptions optional for hot-takes.
stackSummary.titleBanner: Remotion overlay at top 12% of frame — animated 0.4s slide-in at clip start, holds for 2.0s, then fades. Inter Bold 70px, max 2 lines. Hook quote pulled from the picker output.
stackSummary.music: Typically OFF — diegetic voice carries the moment. Optional ElevenLabs Music low instrumental bed at -18dB under the first 2s (banner reveal) only, force_instrumental: true. Never under speech.

constants.structure: 0-2s title-banner reveal → 2-50s the moment plays out → 50-60s payoff or hard cut

variationAxes: source language (en / ru / es / pt / de / fr / etc. — captions and banner follow source; do NOT translate, that's the `podcast-dub` workspace template)
reframe style (smart-crop active speaker / split-screen 2-speakers / static center)
moment type (talkshow guest reveal / interview hot take / panel debate peak / one-liner punchline)
doNotCopyLiterally: Banner copy, caption text, exact moment timestamps. Vibe reference only — every podcast has its own moments.
referenceNotes: Source long-form video is REQUIRED — refuse without it (AGENTS.md hard rule #3). There is no way to invent podcast content; AI-generated 'fake' podcast footage is never acceptable. User supplies a YouTube / TikTok / Instagram / X URL OR an mp4 file. Use `ralphy ref pull --url <url> --slug <name>` to fetch (yt-dlp under the hood). If the user asks for translation/dubbing, redirect to the `podcast-dub` workspace template — different pipeline.
